title: Rich Buttery Tunisian Pistachio Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring ground pistachios and beurre-monte, cooked to perfection in an iron-teppan, this recipe is ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly and offers a buttery, chewy, and nostalgic experience. It is a unique blend of flavors and textures that will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Preheat the iron-teppan over medium heat.
Add the olive oil and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground pistachios and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the beurre-monte and chicken broth, and stir until the beurre-monte is fully incorporated.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
